title: Unit 4 Checkpoint Quiz — Por vs. Para

questions.prompt: Which best describes when to use 'para'?
options: Cause, duration, exchange, means, movement through
Both por and para can be used interchangeably
Para is only used for people, por for things
questions.explanation: 'Para' is forward-looking: destination (where you're headed), purpose (in order to), deadline (by when), recipient (who something is for), opinion (in my view). 'Por' covers cause, duration, exchange, means, and movement through.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: Estudié español ___ tres horas esta mañana.
questions.explanation: 'Por' expresses duration of time. 'Por tres horas' = for three hours. 'Para' would indicate a deadline, not how long something lasted.

options: Necesito el informe para el lunes.
questions.explanation: 'Para el lunes' = by Monday (deadline) → correct use of para. 'Caminé por el parque' (through the park) and 'por cien pesos' (for/at 100 pesos) require por. 'Gracias por' always uses por.

questions.prompt: What does 'Salimos para Veracruz mañana' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Para' + place = destination. 'Salir para' = to leave for (a destination). Compare: 'Pasamos por Veracruz' = we passed through Veracruz (movement through = por).

questions.prompt: 'Para mí, la mejor comida mexicana es el mole.' What does 'para mí' express?
options: In my opinion / from my perspective
The food is for me (I'll eat it)
questions.explanation: 'Para mí' (para + pronoun) expresses personal opinion or perspective: 'In my opinion / For me / As far as I'm concerned'. It's one of the most useful expressions for stating views.

options: Te llamo por teléfono más tarde.
Compré flores por mi mamá (as recipient).
questions.explanation: 'Por teléfono' = by phone (means of communication) → correct. Going to Oaxaca as destination = 'para'. Flowers for mom as recipient = 'para'. Studying in order to learn = 'para aprender'.

questions.prompt: What does 'por las dudas' mean in Mexican Spanish?
questions.explanation: 'Por las dudas' is a common Mexican expression meaning 'just in case'. It's equivalent to 'por si acaso'. Both use 'por' to express a precautionary reason.

questions.prompt: What is the difference between 'Lo hice para ti' and 'Lo hice por ti'?
options: Para ti = I did it for you (you're the beneficiary); Por ti = I did it because of you / for your sake
Para ti = I did it because of you; Por ti = you're the recipient
They mean exactly the same thing
Para is past tense; por is present tense
questions.explanation: This is the classic por/para contrast. 'Para ti' = you are the intended recipient or beneficiary. 'Por ti' = you are the cause or motivation — I did it on your behalf or because of you.
